---
title: "DAFT Package Challenge: Understanding Scurvy"
format: html
execute:
  echo: false
  eval: true
---

# DAFT Package Challenge

## The Story of Scurvy

For centuries, sailors suffered from scurvy on long voyages. In 1747, James Lind discovered that consuming lemons reduced the disease, though the mechanism remained unknown. Later, an incorrect acid theory misled people, until 1928 when Vitamin C deficiency was identified as the true cause. The following DAGs illustrate how our understanding evolved over time.

---

## DAG 1: 1747 Observation – Lemons Helped Sailors

This DAG represents the earliest understanding: lemons seemed to prevent scurvy, without knowing why.  

```{python}
import daft
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

pgm = daft.PGM([4,2], origin=[0,0], dpi=150)
pgm.add_node("lemons","Lemons\nConsumed",1,1,aspect=2.5,scale=1.3,
             plot_params={'facecolor':'#FFFACD','edgecolor':'#DAA520','linewidth':2})
pgm.add_node("scurvy","Scurvy\nReduced",3,1,aspect=2.5,scale=1.3,
             plot_params={'facecolor':'#ADD8E6','edgecolor':'#000080','linewidth':2})
pgm.add_edge("lemons","scurvy")
pgm.render()
plt.title("1747: Observation - Lemons Helped Sailors")
plt.show()
```

DAG 2: Misguided Belief – Acid Theory

Scientists later assumed that the acid in citrus killed bacteria, mistakenly explaining scurvy prevention.

```{python}
pgm = daft.PGM([6,2], origin=[0,0], dpi=150)
pgm.add_node("acid","Acid\n(from citrus)",1,1,aspect=3,scale=1.2,
             plot_params={'facecolor':'#FFE4E1','edgecolor':'#B22222','linewidth':2})
pgm.add_node("bacteria","Bacteria\nDestroyed",3,1,aspect=3,scale=1.2,
             plot_params={'facecolor':'#E6E6FA','edgecolor':'#6A5ACD','linewidth':2})
pgm.add_node("scurvy","Scurvy\nPrevented?",5,1,aspect=3,scale=1.2,
             plot_params={'facecolor':'#B0E0E6','edgecolor':'#00008B','linewidth':2})
pgm.add_edge("acid","bacteria")
pgm.add_edge("bacteria","scurvy")
pgm.render()
plt.title("Misguided Belief: Acid Theory")
plt.show()
```

DAG 3: 1928 Discovery – Vitamin C Prevents Scurvy

The complete explanation revealed Vitamin C deficiency as the true cause of scurvy, correcting earlier misunderstandings

```{python}
import daft
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Create the PGM
pgm = daft.PGM([5,2], origin=[0,0], dpi=150)

# Add nodes
pgm.add_node("vitamin_c", "Vitamin C\n(Intake)", 1.5, 1, aspect=2.8, scale=1.3,
             plot_params={'facecolor':'#98FB98','edgecolor':'#006400','linewidth':2})
pgm.add_node("scurvy", "Scurvy\nPrevented", 3.8, 1, aspect=2.8, scale=1.3,
             plot_params={'facecolor':'#ADD8E6','edgecolor':'#00008B','linewidth':2})

# Add edges
pgm.add_edge("vitamin_c", "scurvy")

# Render DAG
pgm.render()
plt.title("1928: Vitamin C Prevents Scurvy")
plt.show()
```
